user_story: As a subscriber, I want to enable or disable the Fuel fitness plugin so that my assistant gains workout-tracking capabilities.
expected_behaviour: PATCH /api/v1/fuel/settings/ requires JWT auth and a body with `fuel_enabled` (bool). On off→on: creates a FuelProfile (get_or_create), clears the `fuel` key from `tenant.welcomes_sent`, enqueues `apply_single_tenant_config` via QStash, and returns `{fuel_enabled, fuel_profile_status, restart_required: true}` when a container exists. On on→off: sets fuel_enabled=False, bumps pending config, restart_required=true. Returns 400 if `fuel_enabled` is missing, 404 if the user has no tenant. Plugin change requires a container restart — the frontend must call POST /api/v1/fuel/restart/ after user confirms.
inventory_notes: Disabling then re-enabling Fuel clears the welcome delivery marker so the welcome cron re-fires. `restart_required` is only true when a container_id exists. Welcome cron scheduling happens later via /restart/, not here.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to restart my AI assistant after toggling the Fuel plugin so that the plugin is picked up without manual intervention.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/fuel/restart/ requires JWT auth. Calls `restart_container_app(tenant.container_id)` on Azure Container Apps. Returns 400 if no container_id. Returns 502 if Azure restart fails. On success, if fuel_enabled and onboarding_status=pending, enqueues `schedule_fuel_welcome` via QStash with a 90-second delay (so the container boots before the gateway is hit). Returns `{restarted: true}`.
inventory_notes: The 90-second delay is intentional — container cold-start takes ~90s. No restart is attempted if container_id is absent (e.g. unprovisioned tenant).

user_story: As the system, I want to schedule a one-shot welcome message for a newly Fuel-enabled tenant so that they are proactively welcomed without blocking the restart response.
expected_behaviour: The `schedule_fuel_welcome_task` QStash task (dispatched ~90s after container restart) checks that the tenant exists, fuel_enabled=True, and onboarding_status=pending. If so, calls `_schedule_fuel_welcome(tenant)` which delegates to `apps.orchestrator.welcome_scheduler.schedule_welcome` with feature='fuel', cron_name='_fuel:welcome'. The welcome cron fires ~5 minutes after registration. Self-healing: stale fire-past-date welcome crons are replaced. If the prior welcome was delivered (`welcomes_sent.fuel` set), returns early.
dependencies: QStash (task dispatch), OpenClaw container gateway (cron.add), apps/orchestrator/welcome_scheduler.py
inventory_notes: On failure the error is logged but swallowed — the daily `reconcile_welcomes` watchdog retries. The welcome prompt instructs the assistant to mark delivery via POST /api/v1/tenants/runtime/{tenant_id}/welcomes/fuel/ after nbhd_send_to_user succeeds, so failed sends do not stamp delivery.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to view and update my fitness profile so that the assistant knows my goals, equipment, and preferences.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/profile/ returns the FuelProfile for the authenticated tenant (404 if no profile). PATCH /api/v1/fuel/profile/ accepts partial updates for: onboarding_status, fitness_level, goals, limitations, equipment, days_per_week, preferred_days, preferred_time, additional_context, distance_unit. Both require JWT auth. Returns serialized FuelProfile. The consumer-facing endpoint does not update crons (unlike the runtime equivalent).
inventory_notes: distance_unit is always stored in km; the UI converts on display. preferred_time validation accepts morning/afternoon/evening/''. The runtime profile PATCH (OpenClaw) also triggers fuel cron updates when preferred_time changes; the consumer PATCH does not.

user_story: As the OpenClaw assistant, I want to read and update a tenant's fitness profile so that I can conduct onboarding and remember user preferences.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/runtime/{tenant_id}/profile/ and PATCH /api/v1/fuel/runtime/{tenant_id}/profile/ use X-NBHD-Internal-Key + X-NBHD-Tenant-Id auth (validated by validate_internal_runtime_request). PATCH accepts: onboarding_status (pending/in_progress/completed/declined), fitness_level (beginner/intermediate/advanced/''), goals/limitations/equipment (lists of strings), days_per_week (1-7 or null), preferred_days (list of 0-6 ints), preferred_time (morning/afternoon/evening/''). On preferred_time change, if an active plan exists, calls `_manage_fuel_cron(tenant, plan, action='update')` (best-effort). Returns only the profile fields (not healthkit_tombstones or use_session_scheduling). Returns 401 on auth failure, 404 if tenant or profile not found.
dependencies: Postgres (FuelProfile), OpenClaw container gateway (cron.list, cron.remove, cron.add) — best-effort for cron updates
inventory_notes: Profile is get_or_created on PATCH if missing. The PATCH endpoint uses `update_fields` to avoid overwriting unset fields. `use_session_scheduling` and `healthkit_tombstones` are excluded from the serialized response.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to log a completed or planned workout via the web/app so that my fitness history is recorded.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/fuel/workouts/ requires JWT auth. Validates via WorkoutSerializer (date or scheduled_at required; RPE 1-10; detail_json must be object). On create, runs normalize_detail + validate_detail through the set_contract: strength/calisthenics detail_json is validated against typed Pydantic models (WeightedRepsSet, BodyweightRepsSet, HoldTimeSet); returns 400 with LLMValidationError envelope on failure. Source is set by the serializer (defaults to 'user'). After save, detect_prs is called. Returns 201 with full workout. If scheduled_at provided without date, date is derived from tenant timezone.
inventory_notes: post_save signal bumps tenant.fuel_version atomically and (if use_session_scheduling=True) enqueues a 30s-debounced fuel cron regeneration via QStash.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to list my workouts with optional filters so that I can review my training history.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/workouts/ requires JWT auth. Supports query params: category (WorkoutCategory values), status (WorkoutStatus values), date_from, date_to, window (e.g. '4w' or '30d' — rolling window anchored on tenant-local today), limit (default 100, max 500). Results are ordered by -date, -id. Cached 30s per-tenant via @tenant_cache(tag='fuel'). Cache is invalidated on any Fuel write (fuel_version bump).
inventory_notes: window param is ignored if date_from or date_to are also provided. Timezone-aware window calculation uses today_in_tenant_tz to avoid date boundary bugs for non-UTC tenants.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to view, edit, or delete a specific workout so that I can correct errors or add details after the fact.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{workout_id}/ returns the full WorkoutSerializer payload. PATCH accepts partial updates; runs set_contract normalization and validation on detail_json changes; calls detect_prs after save. If PATCH lands on a status=done workout that was already done and was created more than 24h ago, stamps `last_edited_by_user_at` to mark a post-completion edit. DELETE returns 204. All methods return 404 if workout not found for this tenant. JWT auth required.
inventory_notes: Workout rows are fully editable regardless of status (by design — no status gate). A post_delete signal records HealthKit tombstones if external_id is set. post_save bumps fuel_version.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to hold an edit lock on a workout while editing it so that the AI assistant cannot overwrite my in-progress changes.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{workout_id}/edit-lock/ acquires a lock for FUEL_EDIT_LOCK_TTL_SECONDS (default 60s), sets edit_lock_owner='user' and edit_lock_until. Returns `{workout_id, edit_lock_until, edit_lock_owner, ttl_seconds, version}`. The browser heartbeats this at half the TTL. DELETE /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{workout_id}/edit-lock/ releases the lock (idempotent — returns 204 even if already released). Runtime PATCH/DELETE/skip/complete paths check `_edit_locked_response`: if lock is in the future they return 409 with `{error: edit_locked, lock_owner, retry_after_s, edit_lock_until, workout_id}` plus a Retry-After header. JWT auth required for both lock endpoints.
inventory_notes: Lock self-expires after TTL — a browser crash cannot block assistant writes indefinitely. The plan reconciler's apply_reconciliation also respects edit locks (skips deletion of locked workouts). The 409 includes Retry-After even though OpenClaw documents 429 as retry-able — the field is included for forward-compat.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to duplicate a past workout as a new planned session so that I can quickly reuse a workout I have done before.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{workout_id}/duplicate/ requires JWT auth. Looks up the source workout by id+tenant (404 if not found). Creates a new Workout with status='planned', date=today (server UTC date), and copies category, activity, duration_minutes, detail_json. Clears plan, slot, notes, rpe, source. Returns 201 with full WorkoutSerializer payload.
inventory_notes: Date is set to server-side date.today() (UTC), not tenant-local today — potential off-by-one for tenants well ahead of UTC. No plan/slot linkage is carried over.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to mark a planned workout as skipped with an optional reason so that my adherence record is accurate without deleting the session.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{workout_id}/skip/ requires JWT auth. Sets status=skipped, skip_reason (capped to 128 chars). Saves only the changed fields. Returns full WorkoutSerializer. 404 if not found. No edit-lock check (consumer path).
inventory_notes: Distinct from DELETE: preserves the row for adherence calculation. Runtime skip path (OpenClaw) additionally checks edit_lock.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to mark a workout as done with optional notes, RPE, and duration so that my training log is complete.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{workout_id}/complete/ requires JWT auth. Sets status=done; optionally updates notes, rpe (1-10, invalid values silently ignored), duration_minutes (coerced to int). Calls detect_prs after save. Returns full WorkoutSerializer. 404 if not found.
inventory_notes: Full save (not update_fields) — a contrasting design to the runtime complete path which uses scoped update_fields to avoid overwriting HealthKit-synced fields.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to swap two workouts' scheduled times atomically so that I can drag a session to a different day in the calendar.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/fuel/workouts/swap/ requires JWT auth. Body: {a: uuid, b: uuid}. Returns 400 if a or b are missing or identical. Both must belong to the tenant (404 otherwise). Within a DB transaction, swaps scheduled_at, window_start_at, window_end_at, and date between the two rows. Returns `{a: WorkoutSerializer, b: WorkoutSerializer}`.
inventory_notes: The runtime equivalent mirrors this exactly for the assistant path. No plan/slot re-linking occurs on swap — slots retain their original date context.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a lightweight count of my workouts with filters so that the dashboard can show statistics without loading full records.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/workouts/count/ requires JWT auth. Supports category, status, date_from, date_to query params. Returns `{count: int}`. No caching. No limit applied.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see all my workouts grouped by day for a given month so that I can view a calendar heatmap of my training.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/calendar/?year=YYYY&month=MM requires JWT auth and both year (1900-2100) and month (1-12) query params; returns 400 if invalid or missing. Queries all workouts for that month, groups by date, and returns [{date, workouts: [WorkoutStubSerializer]}] sorted by date. Cached 60s per-tenant via @tenant_cache(tag='fuel').
inventory_notes: Uses WorkoutStubSerializer (lightweight: id, date, scheduled_at, category, activity, status, duration_minutes, rpe, plan_id).

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see aggregated progress for a workout category so that I can track improvement over time.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/progress/?category=<cat> requires JWT auth and a valid WorkoutCategory value (400 on invalid). Behaviour by category: strength → per-exercise est1RM trend (Epley formula) over last 365 done workouts; calisthenics → per-skill hold/reps trend; cardio → pace+distance trend + cumulative km; hiit → peak HR trend + totals; mobility/sport/other → session count + total minutes + recent 50 sessions list. All base on status=done. Cached 120s via @tenant_cache(tag='fuel'). Returns `{category, progress}`.
inventory_notes: est_1rm uses the Epley formula: W*(1+R/30), rounded to 1 decimal.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a weekly workout volume summary so that I can see how much I trained in any given week.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/weekly-summary/?week_start=YYYY-MM-DD requires JWT auth. week_start defaults to the most recent Monday (UTC-based). Returns `{week_start, week_end, by_category: [{category, count, total_minutes}], totals: {sessions, minutes}}`. Cached 120s per-tenant. 400 on invalid week_start format.
inventory_notes: Only status=done workouts count. week_start defaults to server-UTC Monday, not tenant-local Monday.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to poll a cheap version counter so that an open workout drawer can detect when the assistant has updated the workout in the background.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/version/ requires JWT auth. Returns `{fuel_version: int}`. The counter is stored on the Tenant model and incremented atomically (F('fuel_version')+1) on every Workout or WorkoutPlan post_save or post_delete signal. The frontend polls this while a workout drawer is open and shows a 'refresh' pill when the counter advances.
inventory_notes: Bump is best-effort (logged on failure but never raises). Also bumped on WorkoutPlan saves/deletes.
